title: 'They are literally everywhere': The shocking story of how forever chemicals polluted the world
summary: Live Science spoke with Mariah Blake, an investigative journalist and author of the book "They Poisoned The World," about one of the greatest corporate scandals in history.

title: Artemis II’s Breathtaking View of the Far Side of the Moon
summary: The first images from Artemis II reveal what the moon looks like just 7,000 km from the surface—and confirm that NASA is ready to return to Earth’s satellite.

title: اعتقال تلميذ ببرشيد لتورطه في قضية تتعلق بالضرب والجرح المفضي للموت
summary: تمكنت عناصر الشرطة بالمنطقة الإقليمية للأمن بمدينة برشید، زوال اليوم الثلاثاء 7 أبريل الجاري، من توقيف تلميذ يبلغ من العمر 17 سنة، وذلك للاشتباه في تورطه في قضية تتعلق بالضرب والجرح المفضي للموت. وحسب المعطيات الأولية للبحث، فقد أقدم التلميذ المشتبه فيه على تعريض تلميذ آخر،أكمل القراءة » المقالة اع…
body: تمكنت عناصر الشرطة بالمنطقة الإقليمية للأمن بمدينة برشید، زوال اليوم الثلاثاء 7 أبريل الجاري، من توقيف تلميذ يبلغ من العمر 17 سنة، وذلك للاشتباه في تورطه في قضية تتعلق بالضرب والجرح المفضي للموت. وحسب المعطيات الأولية للبحث، فقد أقدم التلميذ المشتبه فيه على تعريض تلميذ آخر، أكمل القراءة » المقالة اعتقال تلميذ ببرشيد لتورطه في قضية تتعلق بالضرب والجرح المفضي للموت fمنشورة على جريدة الصباح .

title: 'So much magic': Artemis II shares first images from the far side of the moon, including new 'Earthset' and total eclipse in space
summary: NASA's first set of images captured by the Artemis II crew during their lunar flyby are here, and they're stunnin

title: World's fattest parrot — on the verge of extinction 30 years ago — has record-breaking breeding season
summary: Conservationists are celebrating the 105th kākāpō chick to hatch during the 2026 breeding season — the highest number reported since such records began 30 years ago.

title: Deadly, vivid-green mass sprawls across South African reservoir — Earth from space
summary: A 2022 satellite photo shows a thick mat of blooming algae and invasive aquatic plants spreading across the surface of the reservoir at South Africa's Hartebeespoort Dam. The verdant mass is both toxic and capable of depleting the water's oxygen levels.

title: A Single Strike Won’t Shut Off the Gulf’s Desalination System
summary: The Gulf’s water system is built with layers of backup, but it relies on continuous operation to hold.

title: The Artemis II astronauts have just flown farther from Earth than any humans in history
summary: Artemis II is now the farthest crewed mission from Earth in history. The occasion was marked by a number of poignant moments.

title: Artemis II moon flyby begins: How to watch and what to know
summary: The Artemis II astronauts have reached the moon and will soon lose contact with NASA as they whip around the lunar far side. Here's how to follow along with their journey and everything you need to know about the "dark side" of the moon.

title: 'They could spend 4 or 5 hours per day underwater': How humans adapted to the most challenging environments
summary: In the book "Adaptable," evolutionary anthropologist Herman Pontzer explores human biology and development, and how people have evolved to survive everywhere on Earth.

title: The hungriest black holes in the universe are running out of food, survey of 8,000 cosmic monsters reveals
summary: Astronomers studied 1.3 million galaxies and 8,000 X-ray-spewing supermassive black holes to find out why these gravitational monsters are growing more slowly than ever.

title: Beadnet dress: A 4,500-year-old ancient Egyptian funeral 'gown' that was in vogue during the Old Kingdom
summary: This rare example of an ancient Egyptian beaded dress was likely crafted specifically for a woman's funeral.

title: 5 Mysteries That the Artemis Missions to the Moon Could Finally Solve
summary: The moon is not just a barren rock orbiting the Earth. The Artemis missions could answer the great unknowns that the satellite holds.

title: 'This generation's moment': How the Artemis missions will reframe humanity's relationship with the moon
summary: Live Science spoke with Rebecca Boyle, author of "Our Moon" about how the moon has been viewed both culturally and scientifically through history.

title: Heatbit Maxi Pro Review: a Space Heater That Also Mines Bitcoin
summary: Electricity rates have gotten so atrocious that this Heatbit wants to offset your costs with bitcoin mining. But the math doesn't add up.

title: NASA telescope uncovers new mystery in supernova first spotted by Chinese astronomers 2,000 years ago —‬ Space photo of the week
summary: NASA's Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer reveals the expansion and shock patterns within RCW 86, a supernova observed by early astronomers in A.D. 185.

title: What happened to the Minoan civilization?
summary: The Minoan civilization flourished between roughly 2000 and 1500 B.C. on Crete and nearby islands. How did it come to an end?

title: Octopus quiz: Are you a sucker for cephalopod science?
summary: This quiz tests what you know about octopus anatomy and behavior.

title: I've witnessed nearly 100 rocket launches. Artemis II was like nothing I've ever experienced.
summary: A veteran space reporter describes the full-body experience of watching NASA's historic Artemis II lift off

title: The Trajectory of the Artemis II Moon Mission Is a Feat of Engineering
summary: The astronauts will arrive about 10,300 kilometers beyond our satellite, breaking all previous records for distance from Earth. But how was their route chosen?

title: 'Trust us; you look amazing': Artemis II crewmembers share first message from space
summary: As Artemis II begins humanity's first return to the moon in over 50 years, the four astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft shared their first message from deep space.

title: Homo habilis is the earliest named human. But is it even human?
summary: Between 2 million and 3 million years ago, humans appeared in Africa — but identifying them in the fossil record is turning out to be surprisingly difficult.

title: Rare 'sungrazer' comet MAPS will shine superbright on Saturday ‪—‬ if it survives a dangerous encounter with our star
summary: Comet C/2026 A1 (MAPS) could shine brightly in the evenings after April 4, but only if it survives a very close encounter with the sun.

title: A new tweak to Einstein's relativity could transform our understanding of the Big Bang
summary: A new physics paper proposes modifications to Einstein’s theory of relativity that could solve one of the biggest issues about our understanding of the Big Bang.

title: Artemis II officially leaves Earth's orbit on the way to the moon
summary: With a successful translunar injection burn, NASA's Artemis II mission stopped circling Earth and began the first crewed journey toward the moon since the Apollo era.

title: In photos: Artemis II's historic launch for the moon
summary: Millions watched on April 1 as the Artemis II mission sent humans back to the moon for the first time since 1972. Here's the day in pictures.

title: Earth's energy imbalance is much more extreme than climate models show ‪—‬ but scientists aren't sure why
summary: For reasons that are still unclear, climate models underestimate the growing gap between the amount of energy Earth receives from the sun and the amount the planet radiates into space.

title: Native Americans invented dice and games of chance more than 12,000 years ago, archaeological study reveals
summary: A new study shows that dice and games of chance date back thousands of years earlier than experts previously thought.

title: Artemis II blasts off: Humans are on their way back to the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II rocket has taken off in a historic launch on Florida's Space Coast, sending humans back to the moon for the first time in more than 50 years.

title: Farting comet seen reversing its spin for the first time ever —‬ and it may soon 'self-destruct'
summary: A new analysis of archival Hubble Space Telescope photos reveals that a peculiar comet slowed its spin before fully reversing its rotational direction via "outgassing" during a flyby of Earth in 2017.

title: Extreme wildfires, droughts and storms could happen even under moderate global warming, study finds
summary: New research suggests devastating climate outcomes that are typically associated with extreme global warming could hit even we limit heating to 3.6 F above preindustrial levels.

title: Investigating the reproducibility of the social and behavioural sciences
summary:
Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10203-5

A study of reproducibility in a stratified random sample of 600 papers published from 2009 to 2018 in 62 journals spanning the social and behavioural scienc…
body: Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10203-5 A study of reproducibility in a stratified random sample of 600 papers published from 2009 to 2018 in 62 journals spanning the social and behavioural sciences finds higher reproducibility among more recent papers and papers from journals that require data sharing.

body: Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00965-3 A package of papers looking at the social and behavioural sciences shows the value of researchers collaborating to further the cause of reproducible, replicable and robust findings.

title: Why science has a credibility problem — and how to address it
summary:

body: Nature, Published online: 01 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00972-4 Psychologist Brian Nosek’s latest work indicates that many results in the social sciences don’t stand up to immediate scrutiny. But that’s the beginning of a conversation, not the end, he explains to Nature.

title: 10 ملايين دولار.. مكافأة أميركية لاعتقال شقيقين يقودان عصابة
summary: قالت وزارة الخارجية الأميركية يوم الخميس إنها ستدفع ما يصل إلى 10 ملايين دولار مقابل معلومات تؤدي إلى اعتقال أو إدانة شقيقين تم تحديدهما كزعيمين لعصابة سينالوا المكسيكية في ولاية باها كاليفورنيا، التي تضم مدينة تيخوانا.
